title: Emergency summit called as fuel crisis deepens | 7NEWS
description: The Prime Minister is convening an emergency national cabinet meeting to address Australia's escalating fuel crisis, with plans to appoint a national fuel tsar to manage distribution. Service stations across Sydney's west and northwest are running dry, while South Australia has recorded a 37% increase in fuel drive-offs in the past week, prompting police to call for American-style prepayment systems at petrol stations. title: Australia joins declaration condemning Iran's attacks in Strait of Hormuz
description: World leaders, including Australia, are set to call on Iran to immediately stop blocking the Strait of Hormuz which is leading to petrol shortages and soaring prices around the globe.  An emergency session of the International Maritime Council was ordered on Wednesday where global leaders are expected to condemn Iran’s attacks on seafarers and merchant vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, through which 20 per cent of the world’s oil is shipped. #straitofhormuz #iran #maritime #shippinglane #middleeast

title: Army called in ahead of Cyclone Narelle landfall
description: The army has been put on standby ahead of Tropical Cyclone Narelle's predicted landfall tomorrow morning. The tropical storm has intensified to a Category 4 system, with residents in Far North Queensland bracing for potential flash flooding. #cyclone #cyclonenarelle #tropicalstorm #storm #wildweather

title: Iran's intelligence chief killed in Israeli attack
description: Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib has been killed in the third high-profile assassination in two days.   The country’s Prime Minister, Masoud Pezeshkian, on Wednesday confirmed Israeli reports of a targeted attack which killed Khatib. #iran #middleeast #intelligence #israel #worldnews

title: Iranian soccer player reportedly denies pressure to return to Iran
description: An Iranian Women’s Football player has reportedly spoken out after returning home following the AFC Women’s Asian Cup in Australia., denying there was pressure to come back to Iran. #iran #soccer #lionesses #iranianwomenssoccer #asiacup

title: National fuel coordinator appointed amid supply crisis | 7NEWS
description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has appointed Anthea Harris, former CEO of the Australian Energy Regulator, as Australia's first national fuel coordinator to oversee petrol and diesel supply issues nationwide. The appointment follows an emergency national cabinet meeting in Hobart, where all state and territory leaders agreed to appoint local coordinators to work with Harris. Opposition Leader Angus Taylor criticised the government's handling of fuel supply issues as the ACCC launches an investigation into alleged anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers Ampol, BP and Viva Energy, particularly regarding diesel supply to regional and remote areas. title: Cyclone Narelle intensifies to category 5 | 7NEWS
description: Severe tropical cyclone Narelle has intensified to a Category 5 system and is barrelling towards Queensland's far north, with landfall expected near the remote town of Lockhart River as a Category 4 or 5 system. The Australian Army has been placed on standby, and residents in Cooktown and surrounding areas within the Cook Shire are being urged to shelter in place. This is the most intense cyclone to hit this part of Queensland since 1990, with water supplies being turned off and evacuations underway for those living on boats. title: Iran and Israel exchange strikes over gas field | 7NEWS
description: Israeli-U.S. forces and Iran have exchanged missile attacks in a significant escalation of Middle East tensions. An apartment building in Beirut was flattened after evacuation orders, with Israel claiming it housed Hezbollah funds, while Iran launched retaliatory strikes after Israel hit the Paz natural gas field shared with Qatar. Iran's intelligence minister Ali Laragini has been killed following the earlier assassination of Iran's security chief, prompting new supreme leader Mostaba Khamenei to vow revenge. title: Historic Toowoomba PCYC destroyed by suspicious fire | 7NEWS
description: A historic PCYC youth centre on James Street in Toowoomba was destroyed by fire overnight, with the blaze first spotted by paramedics around 1am. The building, which has served the community since 1969, was unoccupied at the time and no injuries were reported. A man in his 20s is being questioned by police after CCTV footage captured someone fleeing the scene, with the rebuild expected to cost millions of dollars. title: Jacinta Allan faces leadership challenge before election | 7NEWS
description: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan faces potential removal as leader before the November state election, with key figures from her socialist left faction reportedly holding secret discussions about a leadership change. The speculation follows internal polling predicting Labor could lose up to 20 seats, combined with dire polling showing Allan is now the most unpopular leader in the country. If a switch occurs, it would be Victoria Labor's first leadership change this close to an election since John Brumby was replaced by Steve Bracks seven months before Bracks defeated Jeff Kennett. title: Queen Mary's homecoming to Tasmania | 7NEWS
description: Denmark's Queen Mary has returned to her homeland of Tasmania for a royal tour, accompanied by her royal partner. The visit includes a traditional welcome to country ceremony at Government House, tours of a marine conservation farm and electric ship builder Incat, a visit to an award-winning restaurant, a stop at the Museum of Old and New Art (MONA), and a public meet-and-greet along Hobart's waterfront on Hunter Street. title: Sean Penn receives 'Oscar' in Ukraine after skipping ceremony
description: Actor Sean Penn received an Oscar-shaped award made from a railcar damaged in a Russian attack. Ukrainian railway operator Ukrzaliznytsia presented Penn with the award during his visit in Ukraine after he skipped the ceremony  He won an Oscar on Sunday for Best Supporting Actor for his role in One Battle After Another, but didn't attend the ceremony as he was to meet with Zelensky in Kyiv. The long-time advocate for Ukraine has visited the country several times during the four-year war with Russia. #SeanPenn #Oscars #Ukraine
